headline: PCE INFLATION SHOCK — May headline PCE +4.1% YoY (highest since Apr 2023), core +3.4% YoY (highest since Oct 2023) released 8:30 ET; CME July-cut probability collapses below 10%, September probability cut to ~45% from ~62%; NDX futures -1.0%, 10Y yield jumps to 4.62%
summary: The Bureau of Economic Analysis released the May Personal Income and Outlays report Friday 8:30 ET. Headline PCE +4.1% YoY (vs +3.8% April / +3.6% consensus) — the hottest print since April 2023; core PCE excluding food/energy +3.4% YoY (vs +3.1% April / +3.2% consensus) — the hottest core print since October 2023. Driver: U.S./Iran conflict residual energy pass-through plus reaccelerating goods inflation as Section 232 advanced-chip and broader tariff regimes work into PPI. Market reaction: CME FedWatch July-cut probability collapsed from 18.8% (Wednesday post-Powell) to ~8%; September-cut probability cut from ~62% to ~45%; December terminal expectations rose ~12bp. NDX futures -1.0% / S&P futures -0.6% at the print; 10Y yield jumped to 4.62% from 4.49%; dollar bid (DXY +0.4%). Read-through to House coverage: (1) NEGATIVE for long-duration utility/SMR cohort (CEG, VST, GEV, OKLO, SMR, NEE) — these names price the rate-cut path most aggressively; (2) NEGATIVE for high-multiple software (PLTR, NOW, CRM, SNOW) on duration sensitivity — PLTR already at fresh 52-week low; (3) NEGATIVE for AI-neocloud cohort (CRWV, NBIS, IREN, APLD) — duration + financing-cost double-hit; (4) NEUTRAL for hyperscalers (MSFT, AMZN, GOOG, META) — $725B CY26 capex stack is structurally rates-insensitive; (5) the tariff-pass-through component is yellow-flag for NVDA / AMD margin commentary into FQ2 prints — Section 232 25% advanced-chip duties feeding the print.

headline: OPENAI POTENTIALLY DELAYS IPO TO 2027 (NYT) — Altman calls any cut to $1T target valuation 'nonstarter'; SpaceX's June 12 rocky debut cited as cautionary; ORCL slides 1.6%, CRWV -6.31% pre-market, NBIS -9.57% — neocloud financing-overhang re-emerges
summary: The New York Times reported Thursday after market close (via three sources involved in internal deliberations) that OpenAI is now leaning toward delaying its previously H2-2026-targeted IPO until 2027. Key details: (a) Altman is firm that any valuation below the $1 trillion mark is a 'nonstarter'; (b) advisers cautioned the company that current public-tech-market volatility (post-SpaceX's June 12 IPO debut, which traded down ~5% from $135 fixed price to ~$128 close vs perp-market $150-200 indications) may not support a $1T listing; (c) the delay logic is to wait for stronger revenue traction — OpenAI 2026 revenue tracking $13B vs original $20B FY26 plan. Direct market impact: Oracle (ORCL) -1.6% Thursday close on the $300B 2030 datacenter buildout dependency; CoreWeave (CRWV) -6.31% pre-market Friday at $101.32 on its $22B multi-year compute partnership; Nebius (NBIS) -9.57% pre-market at $234.83. Read-through to House coverage: (1) NEGATIVE for ORCL — the $300B OpenAI capex anchor is now visible-but-unfunded for an additional 12 months; the $70B FY27 capex commitment looks more thinly-financed against this revised IPO timeline; (2) NEGATIVE for AI-neocloud cohort (CRWV, NBIS, IREN, APLD) — these names trade on OpenAI's IPO as a financing-validation event; (3) NEUTRAL for NVDA — OpenAI compute commitments don't change, but the H2-2026 'IPO-pull' bullish catalyst is removed; (4) modestly negative for AVGO custom-silicon — OpenAI's first-party ASIC project (Project Stargate compute) timeline shifts.

headline: ON SEMI ACQUIRES SYNAPTICS for $7B in ALL-STOCK — largest deal in onsemi history; 1.350 exchange ratio = ~19% premium to 10-day VWAP; $30B TAM expansion to $243B by 2030 in physical AI / edge compute; ON -15% pre-market on dilution, SYNA +18% pre
summary: ON Semiconductor (ON) announced Friday pre-market the acquisition of Synaptics (SYNA) in an all-stock transaction valued at ~$7 billion — the largest deal in onsemi's history. Each Synaptics share will be exchanged for 1.350 shares of ON common stock, implying ~19% premium to the 10-day volume-weighted average. Strategic rationale: combines onsemi's automotive/industrial silicon franchise with Synaptics' edge-compute platform, wireless connectivity, and human-machine interface portfolio — onsemi explicitly framing the deal as accelerating its 'physical AI' push (robotics, edge inference, sensor fusion). Financial: $30B TAM expansion to $243B by 2030, accretive to non-GAAP EPS within 18 months of close, $200M annual run-rate synergies. Boards of both companies unanimously approved; expected close mid-2027 pending SYNA shareholder vote and regulatory approval. Market reaction: ON tumbled ~15% pre-market on all-stock dilution (~$7B stock to issue = ~10% dilution at current cap); SYNA +18% pre-market reflecting the implied premium. Read-through to House coverage: (1) NOT a tracked AI-Datacenter-Power name catalyst directly, but the deal signals analog/edge-AI is the consolidation battleground for 2026-27 — ADI's $1.5B Empower buy (May 19), STMicro's datacenter pivot, and now ON-SYNA mark a clear trend; (2) marginally negative for STM (Europe analog) and TXN (incumbent edge analog) on competitive heat; (3) signals private valuations in the edge-AI silicon-IP space remain robust despite duration squeeze.

headline: KOSPI -8.18% / SIDECAR CIRCUIT BREAKER TRIPPED — Samsung (005930.KS) -9.21%, SK Hynix (000660.KS) -9.43% — second circuit-breaker rout in four sessions; PCE shock + KRW weakness + foreign outflow wipe out Thursday's +5.42% MU-print rally; KOSPI back to ~8,200 — June 23 lows retested
summary: Korean markets cratered Friday on the PCE inflation shock + KRW weakness. KOSPI plunged 8.18% intraday, triggering its second sidecar circuit breaker in four sessions (June 23 was the prior, also -9.99%). The Tuesday circuit-breaker-and-recover-and-recratter pattern continues: KOSPI is now down ~2.4% on the week despite Thursday's +5.42% Micron-print rally. Samsung Electronics -9.21% to ~KRW 273k; SK Hynix -9.43% to ~KRW 1.81M (fully reversing Thursday's +13% MU-print spike). Foreign outflows resumed aggressively (~KRW 4.2T net sell) on the PCE-driven KRW weakness (USD/KRW back near 1,432). Drivers: (a) U.S. PCE inflation +4.1% / core +3.4% kills July-cut hopes — direct USD-strength shock; (b) KRW depreciation triggers FX-hedge rebalancing flows; (c) two largest KOSPI components (Samsung+SK Hynix combined ~44% of index) bear the brunt; (d) profit-taking on the Thursday short-squeeze rally. Read-through to House coverage: (1) the breadth crack identified June 19 is RE-OPENED — three circuit breakers in 18 trading days marks the most volatile Korean memory cycle since 2008; (2) NEGATIVE for Korean memory complex (005930.KS, 000660.KS) — repeated capitulation creates real holder-base damage even with the structural HBM4 thesis intact; (3) MIXED for MU — Korea weakness modestly supports Hynix-share-loss thesis but exporters lose KRW-translation buffer; (4) sets up adverse open for U.S. AI-infra (NDX futures already -1.0% on PCE) — Asian beta amplifies; (5) the SK Hynix Nasdaq ADR July 10 listing now goes from a relief catalyst to a critical floor event.

headline: MU POST-PRINT PT SCRAMBLE CONTINUES — Melius $2,200 (from $1,100, ~2x doubling); Morgan Stanley $1,200 (from $1,050); Citi $840; Raymond James $1,500; KeyBanc $1,600; TD Cowen $1,500 (from $660 — +127% bump); GS $1,100 (Neutral, from ~$400); RBC $1,500 — new consensus avg rolls to ~$1,540, top three desks at $2,000-$2,200
summary: Second day of Wall Street price target resets on Micron following Tuesday's record Q3 FY26 print ($41.5B rev / 84.9% GM / $25.11 EPS / $50B Q4 guide / $100B SCAs). Notable Friday-pre-open updates beyond Thursday's JPM/Susquehanna/Barclays/DA Davidson wave: (a) Melius Research's Ben Reitzes — DOUBLED PT to $2,200 from $1,100, naming the SCA disclosure 'the single most important structural pivot in MU history'; (b) Morgan Stanley to $1,200 from $1,050, Overweight; (c) Citi to $840 (relatively conservative); (d) Raymond James $1,500; (e) KeyBanc $1,600; (f) TD Cowen $1,500 from $660 (+127% — largest single-firm bump); (g) RBC Capital $1,500; (h) Goldman Sachs $1,100 from ~$400 — but REMAINS NEUTRAL on valuation/timing — Schneider's commentary acknowledges the SCA disclosure is 'positive for multiple stability' but stops short of upgrade. New consensus average PT now ~$1,540 (from ~$865 pre-print); top-end $2,000-$2,200 from Melius, Susquehanna, Barclays, DA Davidson. Stock action: MU -4.7% pre-market Friday on profit-taking after Thursday's +14.6% AH and +18% high-tick. Read-through to House coverage: (1) the implied bull-case EPS power supporting $2,200 PTs at 25-30x is $73-88/share — implies FY27 EPS run-rate well above current consensus $100; (2) MU is now the third AI-infra name (after NVDA, AVGO) with a fully-converged consensus bull case; (3) sympathetic positive for SK Hynix, Samsung — but Korea PT scrambles paused by today's circuit-breaker rout; (4) memory-WFE (AMAT, LRCX, KLAC, ASML) thesis intact; (5) GS-as-last-bear position increasingly untenable.

headline: AI-NEOCLOUD COMPLEX TAKES DOUBLE-HIT — CRWV -6.31% pre-market at $101.32, NBIS -9.57% at $234.83, IREN/APLD -4-6% — PCE-driven duration squeeze layered on top of OpenAI IPO delay financing-overhang; CRWV now -26% from early-May peak, NBIS hits ATH Monday then -12% in four sessions
summary: The AI-neocloud cohort is taking a double hit Friday pre-market: (a) PCE +4.1% / core +3.4% drives the entire long-duration complex sharply lower as terminal-rate expectations reset higher; (b) NYT report that OpenAI may delay its IPO to 2027 removes the H2-2026 'IPO-pull' catalyst the entire neocloud trade was front-running. Specifics: CoreWeave (CRWV) -6.31% pre at $101.32 — now -26% from its early-May peak ($137); Nebius (NBIS) -9.57% pre at $234.83 — having hit its all-time high Monday at $267 before rolling over; IREN -5% pre; Applied Digital (APLD) -6% pre. Concentration risk: CRWV's $22B multi-year OpenAI partnership and the broader $300B Oracle/OpenAI 2030 buildout that anchors the secondary GPU-cloud financing market are now visibly thinner. The NDX-100 rebalance EFFECTIVE June 22 added CRWV/NBIS — they're now passive-flow-vulnerable to broader NDX-100 selloff. Read-through to House coverage: (1) HIGH-SEVERITY watchlist signal — financing-risk re-emerges for the entire neocloud cohort; (2) the GPU-collateral debt market (CRWV $3.1B DDTL Ba2/BB+, MS-led EUR HY roadshow June 10) faces refinancing-cost reset as 10Y crosses 4.6%; (3) sympathetic NEGATIVE for NVDA-underwritten secondary GPU buyers (Sharon AI, Lambda Labs private rounds); (4) marginally positive for hyperscalers (MSFT/AMZN/GOOG/META) — first-party capex doesn't depend on neocloud financing channels.

headline: PLTR HITS FRESH 52-WK LOW $108 (-2.74% close $113.50) — longest consecutive daily losing streak since March 2023; sovereign contract threats in Europe, EU AI Act scrutiny, valuation compression from 200x P/E peak; -32% from May high
summary: Palantir Technologies (PLTR) closed Thursday at $113.50 (-2.74%) and tagged a fresh 52-week intraday low of $108 — marking its longest consecutive daily losing streak since March 2023 (12 sessions). Stock is now -32% from its May high of $167. Drivers: (1) duration squeeze — 10Y rising to 4.62% on PCE shock punishes the 180-200x forward P/E multiple most; (2) sovereign-contract threats in Europe escalating — France's Mistral lobby pushed for EU AI Act Article 6(2) carve-outs that would restrict U.S.-domiciled defense-AI suppliers; (3) competitive dynamics — Anthropic's federal commercial wins (Claude Gov reference customer rollout) eroding Palantir's incumbent moat; (4) thesis-vs-multiple debate intensifying despite Q1 FY26 revenue acceleration (+39% YoY) and AIP customer count growth. Read-through to House coverage: (1) HIGH-SEVERITY overvalued signal flag — PLTR is the cleanest 'duration AI-software short' on the long-rate-back-up trade; (2) marginally negative read-across to other high-multiple software (NOW, SNOW, CRM, MDB) — though those names trade at 12-18x forward sales vs PLTR's 40x+; (3) NEUTRAL for NVDA / AVGO / hyperscalers — infrastructure capex is multiple-stage decoupled from PLTR's enterprise software bid.

headline: SK HYNIX (000660.KS) NASDAQ ADR LISTING — formal pricing window TARGETED JULY 10; $29.4B raise via 17.79M new shares (KRW 45.45T); BofA / Citi / GS / JPM joint lead; structurally re-rates $1T+ memory-cycle leader to U.S. passive-flow base — but Korea rout creates pricing risk
summary: SK Hynix's $29.4 billion Nasdaq ADR listing — formally board-approved June 24 with all six outside directors attending — remains targeted for trading debut Tuesday July 10 (subject to SEC effectiveness). Mechanics: third-party allotment of up to 17.79 million new common shares represented as ADRs (1 share = 10 ADRs), raising up to KRW 45.45 trillion (~$29.4B). Joint lead managers: BofA Securities, Citigroup, Goldman Sachs, JP Morgan. Use of proceeds: 70%+ for HBM4/HBM4E wafer capacity expansion at M16/M15X fabs in Cheongju, plus ASML EUV scanner procurement (next-gen High-NA shipments). The listing is the largest Korean equity issuance in history and the largest ADR offering since Alibaba 2014. Critical Friday-AM tape concern: today's Seoul circuit-breaker rout (Hynix -9.43%) puts pricing risk on the deal — the indicative ADR price band was set against KRW 1.92M / share equivalent (Wednesday close); current spot is KRW 1.81M. Read-through to House coverage: (1) STRONG STRUCTURAL POSITIVE for 000660.KS even with near-term Korea volatility — the U.S. passive-flow base addition (S&P Global BMI, MSCI ACWI ex-USA, FTSE Global) creates permanent incremental demand; (2) re-rate driver for the memory-cycle thesis: Hynix at $1T+ market cap on dual-listed flow could close part of the MU-Hynix valuation discount; (3) MARGINALLY NEGATIVE for MU near-term — second large memory equity supply hits the market; (4) memory-WFE positive — proceeds go directly to ASML / Applied / Lam / Tokyo Electron order books.

headline: GE VERNOVA (GEV) launches GridOS for Transmission + breaks ground on $105M Advanced Research Center, Niskayuna NY — orchestration software for hyperscaler-driven grid load + Direct Air Capture R&D on gas turbines; AI-power picks-and-shovels thesis intact despite duration squeeze
summary: GE Vernova announced Thursday (a) the GridOS for Transmission orchestration platform — a software stack to help utilities sequence and integrate fast-growing datacenter loads onto constrained transmission grids, targeting the hyperscaler-PPA-procurement bottleneck identified in the June Carnegie Endowment study; (b) groundbreaking on a $105 million Advanced Research Center at the company's flagship Niskayuna, NY campus, pursuing carbon reduction via Direct Air Capture (DAC) on gas-powered engines plus next-gen turbine efficiency. Strategic context: GEV's H-class gas turbine fleet now tops 4M operating hours; the 110 GW year-end-2026 gas-turbine backlog + slot reservation target was reaffirmed by CEO Strazik. Q1 FY26 orders were $18.3B (+71% organic); CY26 datacenter orders already exceed CY25 total. Read-through to House coverage: (1) POSITIVE for GEV — software-and-services optionality adds margin layer to the gas/grid build-out; (2) the picks-and-shovels cohort (GEV, VRT, ETN, GLW, ANET) remains the LOWEST-BETA way to express the $725B CY26 hyperscaler capex thesis — even with today's PCE-driven duration squeeze, the order-book visibility through 2027 is rate-insensitive; (3) sympathetic positive for Eaton (ETN) — grid software/hardware adjacency on transmission orchestration; (4) confirms the Lambda/Carnegie thesis that gas-turbine power fills 2026-27 demand while nuclear PPAs serve 2027+ — gas-and-grid is the near-duration bottleneck.
